title: General Studies Paper I
syllabus: Indian Heritage and Culture, History and Geography of the World and Society.

questions.text: Discuss the main features of the urban planning of the Indus Valley Civilization.
What were the factors that led to the rise of Magadha as the foremost Mahajanapada?
Evaluate the contribution of the Bhakti and Sufi movements to the composite culture of India.
Examine the role of trade and commerce in the urbanization of India during the medieval period.
Discuss the evolution and significance of Gandhara art in the context of cultural exchanges in ancient India.
What were the main features of the architecture of the Vijayanagara Empire?
Discuss the significance of the Revolt of 1857 in the history of India's freedom struggle.
Analyze the impact of colonial land revenue systems on Indian agriculture.
Explain the mechanism of the Indian monsoon and its variability. How does it affect Indian agriculture?
Discuss the contributions of the Pallavas to South Indian art and architecture.
Examine the role of women in the Indian National Movement.
Discuss the major physiographic divisions of India and their economic significance.
Evaluate the nature and significance of the Mauryan art and architecture.
Discuss the salient features of the Indian society and the challenges of social transformation.
Discuss the main teachings of Buddhism and its impact on Indian society.
Examine the geographical factors that influenced the location of major industrial regions in India.
